claimText: Functional de-extinction aims to revive the ecological roles and traits of extinct species rather than creating exact genetic replicas.
evidenceExcerpt: Functional de-extinction focuses on reviving the ecological roles and traits of extinct species rather than creating exact replicas.
examUse: Statement-based question on the definition and objective of functional de-extinction.
distractorHooks: Creates exact genetic replicas of extinct species
Focuses solely on preserving DNA in cryogenic banks

claimText: Techniques utilized in functional de-extinction include CRISPR gene editing, cloning, and genome mapping.
evidenceExcerpt: Techniques include CRISPR gene editing, cloning, and genome mapping.
examUse: Multiple-choice question identifying technologies associated with de-extinction.

claimText: The first successful functional de-extinction involved genetically engineering grey wolf pups to carry traits of the extinct dire wolf.
evidenceExcerpt: Colossal Biosciences engineered grey wolf pups to carry traits of the extinct dire wolf.
examUse: Fact-based statement in a multi-statement question regarding recent biotechnology milestones.
distractorHooks: Cloned an exact replica of a dire wolf from fossilized DNA
Engineered dire wolves to carry traits of modern grey wolves

claimText: Planned candidates for functional de-extinction include the wooly mammoth, moa, thylacine, and dodo.
evidenceExcerpt: Other planned candidates for functional de-extinction include the wooly mammoth, moa, thylacine, and dodo.
examUse: List-based question on species targeted for functional de-extinction.
